message:
Fix Mermaid graph node ID collisions and escaping

The graph export command could produce invalid Mermaid diagrams when
node IDs collided after character sanitization (e.g., two prompts
whose IDs differ only in special characters would get the same
Mermaid node ID after stripping non-alphanumeric characters).

Changes:
- Append SHA-256 hash suffix to Mermaid node IDs to guarantee
  uniqueness even when sanitized base strings collide
- Truncate base ID portion to 20 chars to keep diagram readable
- Fix Mermaid label escaping: use #quot; entity instead of \"
  (backslash escaping is not valid in Mermaid labels)
- Strip brackets/parens/braces from labels to prevent Mermaid
  syntax conflicts (these characters have special meaning in
  Mermaid node shape declarations)
- Remove the makeUniqueId deduplication wrapper — the hash-based
  approach makes it unnecessary
- Use crypto.createHash for deterministic ID generation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
